name: Govind Mittal
status: Open to senior AI / research roles, research collaborations, and freelance / consulting work.
one_liner: I build autonomous, agentic AI systems — and study what happens inside them.
about: Govind Mittal is a Senior Data Scientist / AI Engineer who designs and ships production agentic-AI systems. He is currently at Gartner (agentic AI for client-call intelligence), and was previously a Senior AI Scientist at Eli Lilly (agentic tooling for Finance) and a Senior Data Scientist at Citigroup (ML, forecasting and risk). His core strengths are agentic AI — LangGraph multi-agent orchestration, RAG and Graph-RAG, LLM fine-tuning, and evaluation/observability — paired with deep AI/agent behavioural intuition (predicting why agents fail or succeed). He has an MSc in Economics (Gokhale Institute, batch prize in Econometrics) and a BSc in Economics & Statistics (NMIMS), so his work blends rigorous quantitative thinking with hands-on AI engineering.
what_he_is_looking_for: Senior AI engineer / AI scientist / research-engineer roles at top tech companies and frontier AI labs
AI research collaborations (agents, mechanistic interpretability, time-series/forecasting)
Freelance / consulting on agentic AI and automation (manufacturing, medical, digital marketing)
interests_and_goals: Long-term, Govind wants to do fundamental AI research — training dynamics, new architectures, and mechanistic interpretability — ideally at a frontier lab or a top PhD program, and to build deep-tech that solves real problems (especially in India). He is also drawn to quantitative finance and to building an AI/automation consulting practice.
education: MSc Economics — Gokhale Institute of Politics & Economics, Pune (batch prize: Econometrics, Business Analytics, Linear Economics)
BSc Economics & Statistics — NMIMS, Mumbai (Best Paper Presentation; Budding Economist award)
